title: Jos Vondar
content:
{{Top|leg}}

{{quote|Sometimes I feel like finding whoever started this rankweed war and performing a pneumonectomy with my bare hands|Jos Vondar|<ref name="JH">''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]''</ref>}}
'''Jos Vondar''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Doctor/Legends|surgeon]] who served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as [[chief medical officer]] of [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. While stationed at [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7]] on [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]], he forged a close friendship with his roommate, [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] surgeon [[Zan Yant]], as well as [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] [[Den Dhur]], [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[I-5YQ]], [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], [[Equani]] therapist [[Klo Merit]] and—after Zan Yant's unfortunate [[Death/Legends|death]]—with his roommate [[Kornell Divini|Kornell 'Uli' Divini]]. Yet the most important relationship he formed while on Drongar was with the [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] [[Nurse/Legends|nurse]] [[Tolk le Trene]], with whom he fell in love. By falling in love and [[Marriage/Legends|marrying]] an [[Ekster]] (a non-Corellian), he was shamed in the eyes of his culture. Even [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Erel Kersos]], Jos’ great-uncle, tried to intervene in the relationship to avoid that his great-nephew didn’t go through the same trauma he had, yet Vondar stood his ground and convinced him of otherwise.

As the [[Battle of Drongar|battles on Drongar]] were coming to a head, Vondar proved himself to be a hero by [[Death/Legends|killing]] [[Klo Merit]], who was discovered to be a double-agent [[Spy/Legends|spy]] who had infiltrated their Rimsoo and was working for both the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] and the criminal organization [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]]. He also found an unexpected support in his great-uncle [[Erel Kersos|Erel]], who wanted to return with him to [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and [[Tolk le Trene|Tolk]], to help them build a relationship against the family tradition.

==Biography==
[[File:CorelliaLand.jpg|thumb|left|350px|Jos was raised on Corellia]]
Jos Vondar was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] and raised in a small [[Farm/Legends|farm]] [[City/Legends|town]] on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|310|Vondar, Dr. Jos}}</ref> in a temperate zone where the weather was pleasant most of the [[Standard year/Legends|year]], and even during the [[Water/Legends|rainy]] season it was mild. There were a lot of [[Doctor/Legends|doctors]] in his family, and several surgeons; the [[Vondars]] and the [[Kersos]], his [[Parent/Legends|father]]’s and mother’s clans, were very solidly [[Ensterite|enster]], disciples of a long and traditional sociopolitical affiliation in which Jos had also been raised. A big part of an enster’s core belief system was that no [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] could be made, much less consummated, outside the inhabitants of one’s own [[Star system/Legends|planetary system]].<ref name="BS" /> At six years of age, Jos had never been off-world, and the only sighting of [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] he’d had were at a distance. So when the subject of outlanders came up in the school rec-dome, it had been puzzling to him. He had asked his father about it on one of the rare evenings in which he had been home and not working at the clinic. It had taken him some time to work up the courage to approach him. His father was never violent, and Jos had no doubt that the man loved him. But he was ''big''; when he stood, he towered over Jos. And he could be loud, very loud, though never when he was talking to his son. In retrospect, it was clear that his father had not been ready for that conversation. What Jos recalled of the time was that, once he had approached and told him about his schoolmates’ talk, his father had stopped whatever he was doing and took his time on trying to explain to Jos in a ludic way the complexities of their family tradition. Only by living the paradigm himself, [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] later, Jos finally had a much better idea of what his father meant.<ref name="JH" /> When he was twenty, Vondar left home to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], and became a student at medical school. Subsequently, he did his internship at the “Big Zoo”, the unofficial name of [[Galactic Polysapient Medical Center|Galactic Polysapient]], the multi-[[Sentience/Legends|sentient]]-[[Species/Legends|species]] [[Medcenter/Legends|medcenter]] on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]].<ref name="JH" /> 

==Serving the Republic==
By the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Jos was stationed on [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]], attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and was named [[Chief Surgeon]] at [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7|Rimsoo Seven]], reporting to [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[D'Arc Vaetes]].<ref name="CSWE" /> Vondar didn’t have any believe in the [[The Force/Legends|Force]] until he met [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], who was stationed at Rimsoo Seven some two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]]. He knew very little about it – not even how to test for it, because that knowledge was supposedly reserved for the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]]. He was aware of the power of the mind-body connection, of course, but he had no talents in that direction. Though he’d heard about the Force all his life, he’d never really believed such a thing could exist until he met the Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], who not only demonstrated to him in locum and in real time, but also saved him in another occasion, as well as challenged his credo regarding the [[Human/Legends|“human”]] nature of the [[Cloning/Legends|clones]]. Jos had seen evidence of individuation, but only in areas that didn’t interfere with their ability to fight, or their loyalty to the Republic. He’d often heard them referred to disparagingly as “meat [[Droid/Legends|droids]]”…he didn’t care for the term, but as a description, it seemed apt. Nevertheless, after his brief but impactful encounters with [[CT-914]] – and his subsequent demise – assisted by his appointments with the [[Empath]] [[Klo Merit]], who also challenged the chief surgeon’s beliefs about highly advanced [[Artificial intelligence|artificial living]] forms – like the one of [[I-5YQ]] - Jos’ view of life was gradually shaken up and turned into an existential conundrum he hardly could wrestle with.<ref name="BS" />

Additionally, as the culmination of all his personal dilemmas, Jos Vondar found himself physically and emotionally attracted to the [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] [[Nurse/Legends|nurse]] [[Tolk le Trene]], even though he knew any relationship they could have would go against his [[Ensterite]] background. The more extreme zealots restricted it even further, refusing to allow any affiliations offplanet. No exceptions were made. Yes, a young [[Gender/Legends|man]] or woman could go offworld, and yes, even the staunchest Ensterites might turn a blind eye if a son or a daughter somehow managed a temporary alliance with one of the [[Ekster]] – the “outsiders” – it wasn’t spoken of in polite circles, but it was done. However, one could ''not'' bring an ekster home to meet one’s [[Parent/Legends|parents]]. ''That'' was simply not done.<ref name="JH" />

Despite the fact [[Tolk le Trene|Tolk]] indeed offered him the chance of a relationship, even if it was brief and uncommitted, Jos did not like nor was he comfortable with the idea of brief liaisons. He decisively changed his mind, nonetheless, after the tragic [[Death/Legends|death]] of his co-worker and friend [[Zan Yant]] – a dedicated [[Doctor/Legends|surgeon]] and a wonderful [[Music/Legends|musician]] – and decided to engage on a full committed love affair with the [[Nurse/Legends|nurse]].<ref name="BS" /> Consequently, their relationship was put to the test when Jos’ great-uncle, [[Admiral/Legends|Admira]] [[Erel Kersos]], was positioned as the new [[Commanding officer/Legends|Commanding Officer]] of the [[MedStar-class frigate/Legends|''MedStar''-class frigate]], replacing [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Tarnese Bleyd]] after his murder by an [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]]. An Ensterite surgeon like Jos himself, coming from his [[Parent/Legends|mother]]’s clan, speaker of the [[High Tongue]] inclusively, Erel’s name wasn’t totally unfamiliar to Jos. Somewhere during his boyhood, he and a cousin had discovered in the family archives fragments of broken [[Hologram/Legends|holograms]] – shattered images of, among others, the young man who had thrown away his heritage and been disowned by the family he chose to abandon. By all that was strict and proper, Jos knew he wasn’t allowed to address Erel Kersos at all, save as a military subordinate replying to a superior [[Officer/Legends|officer]]. Great-Uncle Erel was still a non-''permes'' – the social and personal invisibility did not diminished with time, or even with death. Yet, because of the relationship he chose to engage with an ekster female and his determination to keep it that way, the prohibition against speaking to a shunned relative didn’t seem quite such a major infraction. Until Kersos himself decided to meddle with his great-nephew’s relationship.<ref name="JH" />

Determined to “spare him of grief” due to the trauma of his own personal experience, [[Erel Kersos|Erel]] had a private conversation with [[Tolk le Trene]] on the matter, and explained the situation regarding his and Jos’ culture in such a way that it convinced her to request for a transfer to [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 3|Rimsoo 3]], across the [[Sea of Sponges]], a thousand [[Kilometer/Legends|klicks]] north away from Jos. At first, Vondar could not fathom why Tolk left him until later on, after [[Standard day/Legends|days]]under high stress and fighting depression, in a moment of epiphany, he realized how his great-uncle intervened in his relationship with the nurse, and in an exasperated move, he went to see [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Kersos in his office abord the [[MedStar-class frigate/Legends|Medstar]], demanding explanations for his inconvenient act. After a severe debate in which Jos was, in several moments, at the verge of physically assaulting his great-uncle, and even the state of the Ensterite culture and values were put to the test, with Jos eventually bringing up how the practice of [[Hustru fönster]] became more widespread in their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Erel - who mirrored his younger self in Jos in that moment - decided to support his great-nephew’s choice, and corrected his mistake by having Tolk transferred back to [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7|Rimsoo 7]]. After the [[Battle of Drongar]], convinced his family’s beliefs were not his anymore, Jos chose to [[Marriage/Legends|marry]] Tolk. He returned to [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and, with the support of his great-uncle Erel Kersos, decided to face his family on at least trying to reason with them over his decision.<ref name="JH" />

==Facing moral battlefields==
Jos Vondar’s medical ethics were put to the test as well during his service on [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]]. Only a couple of [[Local day/Legends|days]] after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of his friend [[Zan Yant]], he had to operate on an enemy combatant – a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], who was also a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] – [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Sar Omant]], of the [[Freelance Mercenary Corps/Legends|Freelance Mercenary Corps]]. Vondar later found out that Omant was the commander of the [[Sar Omant's strike force|strike force]] of bios and [[Droid/Legends|droids]] who led the charge against their [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit/Legends|Rimsoo]] camp – which resulted on Zan’s death. With Sar’s sarcastic comments and total lack of repentance, and the operation itself considered of high risk – thirty, maybe forty percent chance of survival – Jos fiercely struggled between his grief and anger of somehow “doing justice” for Zan’s death by letting the Zabrak mercenary die, and his medical code of honor and conduct, as well as his consciousness as a person. Even [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], who [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] Vondar’s wrath through the [[The Force/Legends|Force]], tried to intervene in a way, in order to avoid that the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Doctor/Legends|surgeon]]’s darkest impulses maculated his deeds. Eventually, all the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|padawan]] could do was, watching over from afar through the Force, to regulate the [[Anesthetic/Legends|anesthesia]] given to Sar Omant, who was fairly resistant to it. Ultimately, after a brief but profound and edifying conversation with the [[Minder (occupation)|minder]] [[Klo Merit]] in the [[Refresher/Legends|refresher], Jos let go of his anger and reflected that the best way to serve his friend’s memory was by doing what he did best: saving lives.<ref name="MSI">{{InsiderCite|83|MedStar: Intermezzo}}</ref>     
[[File:Josandsar.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Jos with patient [[Sar Omant]].]]

As the [[Battle of Drongar|battles on Drongar]] were coming to a head, after several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] of investigation, [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Bariss Offee]] concluded that, in a process of elimination, the [[Spy/Legends|spy]] behind the [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] acts in their camp was [[Klo Merit]]. The [[Equani]] [[Empath]] had infiltrated their [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit/Legends|Rimsoo]] and was working for both the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] and the criminal organization [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] with the codenames of Column and Lens, respectively. Soon after this revelation, Jos Vondar went personally to face Merit, who was on the run, at first pretty decided to shoot him to [[Death/Legends|kill]]. But after listening to Klo’s testimony and motivations – the undoing of his [[Equanus|homeworld]] being the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]’s fault, which never took any accountability for – Jos hesitated for a while. Following a debate in which both [[Doctor/Legends|doctors]] were measuring the pain and grief of losing one being in comparison to losing billions, Vondar concluded that killing Merit wouldn’t bring anyone he cared back, but he couldn’t let him go either, and so he fired at him. In the wake of an explosion and his subsequent unconsciousness, Jos woke up at the [[Medical bay|Sickbay One]] on [[MedStar-class frigate/Legends|Medstar]], as he was treated by [[Kornell Divini|Lieutenant Divini]], [[Tolk le Trene]] and Bariss Offee. He was congratulated by both his superiors [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[D'Arc Vaetes]] and [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Erel Kersos]], as well as his fellow colleagues, for doing the right thing. Despite his personal conflict triggered by the fact that he as a doctor, had killed a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] being on purpose - regardless it was the right thing to do – all Jos Vondar could do was to learn how to deal with that fact for the rest of his life.<ref name="JH" />

==Personalities and traits==
{{quote|I guess I’m just not a very introspective sort. My family and clan are big on tradition, not communication. My dad’s idea of revealing moment is forgetting to lock the ‘fresher.|Jos Vondar to [[Klo Merit]]|<ref name="BS" />}}
Jos Vondar was known to be a formidable [[Doctor/Legends|surgeon]]; he knew how to slice and splice the innards of a dozen [[Species/Legends|species]], including his own. That was his talent, his gift, and he was very good at it. So good that at times he felt almost bored with the routine plumbing repairs he had to make, for the most part, on the [[Cloning/Legends|clones]]. He very rarely lost one, and when he did, due to sepsis or hidden trauma or some other nasty surprise, it was hard to feel too much grief. Consequently, like so many others in his profession, he was a pragmatist – he believed in what was real, what was quantifiable and measurable.<ref name="BS" /> 

Additionally, Jos was somewhat a skeptical and cynical individual. The dire and calamitous conditions of his work on [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]] as well as the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] overall made him learn pretty quickly how to cope with whatever was wrong at the moment and not worry about future problems until he had to; the mental equivalent of a triage. Due to his overwhelming workload and lack of any regular sleeping schedule, one of the very little entertainments he had – and perhaps coping mechanisms - was [[Alcohol/Legends|drinking]] and playing [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] in [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7|Rimsoo 7]]’s only [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] with his co-workers and friends. It was a way of relaxing and rebuilding for the next onslaught of [[Blood/Legends|blood]] and pain, as well as for the next [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] or two being able to disconnect from the war.<ref name="JH" />
[[File:Medstar.jpg|right|thumb|Vondar plays Sabacc on Drongar.]]

Furthermore, Vondar wasn’t a very meditative kind of person, although he presented, not infrequently, signs of wistfulness or melancholic periods. Mostly due to the conditions of his environment yet also, and especially, due to his personal dilemma triggered by him falling in love with the [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] [[Nurse/Legends|nurse]] [[Tolk le Trene]], and ultimately deciding to be with her. Because of his [[Ensterite]] background, his relationship with Tolk was technically forbidden. Even though on his [[Corellia/Legends|world]] it was acceptable – barely – that a young unmarried person might go forth into the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] and sample the pleasures of [[Ekster]] company. It wasn’t spoken of in polite circles, but it was done. Then the young, having got it out of their systems, were to return home, find a spouse from a proper enster family, and settle down. But even in his younger and wilder days, Jos had never been comfortable with the idea of brief liaisons. He’s done it, of course, but the essentially meaningless encounters had weighed heavily upon him; at the core of his being, Jos knew that there would only be one love in his life, and that he should not be unfaithful to her – even if he did so before he ever met her.<ref name="BS" /> 
He concluded that, despite his rotations on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], during which dozens of [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] had been laid open before him, even though he no longer spoke the [[High Tongue]] or observed the [[Purging Days]] – even then, though he fancied himself fairly galactopolitan… the interdiction, the barrier between his kind and all others, had worked for him on a deep level, so deep he hadn’t even realized its power…until he decided to finally break away from it. Jos not only acknowledged that his persona should not be predefined by his family name or culture, but also understood that his choices should not be measured by a credo he ceased to believe in.<ref name="JH" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|I've always imagined him as the Star Wars equivalent of Hawkeye Pierce.|Daniel Wallace, on Jos Vondar|<ref name="Wallace blog">{{Blog|danwallace|52|Endnotes for The New Essential Guide to Droids: PART 1 OF 3}}</ref>}}

Jos Vondar was created by authors [[Michael Reaves]] and [[Steve Perry]] as the primary protagonist of the [[2004]] [[MedStar Duology|''Medstar'' Duology]] of novels, [[MedStar I: Battle Surgeons|''Battle Surgeons'']] and [[MedStar II: Jedi Healer|''Jedi Healer'']].<ref name="BS" /><ref name="JH" /> The duology was envisioned as ''[[Star Wars]]''{{'s}} answer to the [[Wikipedia:M*A*S*H|''M*A*S*H'']] [[Wikipedia:MASH (film)|film]] and [[Wikipedia:M*A*S*H (TV series)|television series]],<ref name="Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' {{C|under "Star Wars: Medstar"}}</ref> and it was pitched to Reaves and Perry as "''M*A*S*H'' in space."<ref name="Reaves interview 2">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theclonewarz.net/features/interviews/michaelreaves2.htm|text=New Michael Reaves Interview|work=The Unofficial Clone Wars Site|archivedate=20130401140201}}</ref> Both of them were large fans of both the movie and TV show and felt that doctors-during-war were interesting characters<ref name="Perry interview">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theclonewarz.net/features/interviews/steveperry.htm|text=Meet the Medstar Creative Team - Steve Perry, co-author|work=The Unofficial Clone Wars Site|archivedate=20130619183914}}</ref>—Perry additionally had a medical background<ref name="Reaves interview 1">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theclonewarz.net/features/interviews/michaelreaves.htm|text=Meet the Medstar Creative Team - Michael Reaves, co-author|work=The Unofficial Clone Wars Site|archivedate=20130619183909}}</ref>—and they made an effort to design characters who felt real, with personal problems that needed to be solved.<ref name="Perry interview" /> Jos Vondar in particular has been compared by fellow ''Star Wars'' author [[Daniel Wallace]] to [[Wikipedia:List_of_M*A*S*H_characters#Hawkeye_Pierce|Hawkeye Pierce]], the protagonist of the ''M*A*S*H'' franchise.<ref name="Wallace blog" /> In a review of ''Battle Surgeons'' posted on [[TheForce.net]], reviewer Mike Cooper stated that he found it impossible to read any of Vondar's lines without hearing the voice of [[Wikipedia:Alan Alda|Alan Alda]], the actor who played Hawkeye in the ''M*A*S*H'' TV series.<ref name="Battle Surgeons Review">{{WebCite|url=http://theforce.net/books/reviews/r_medstar1.asp|text=Medstar I: Battle Surgeons by Michael Reaves and Steve Perry|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20130117174928}}</ref>

Throughout the second half of ''Jedi Healer'', Vondar's name is misspelled "Vandar" in almost every appearance, until the epilogue when it is once again correctly spelled.<ref name="JH" />
